text.title: Niche Scoring in Idea Generation Methods
text.summary: 5-dimension niche scorecard (market/competition/barriers/monetization/fit), decision thresholds, and fabrication risk rules for agent scoring.

section.title: Scoring dimensions
section.p: Score each idea 1-5 on five dimensions (max 25 total):

list.item: Competition: 1-2 = red ocean 10+ competitors, 3-4 = moderate 3-10, 5 = blue ocean <3
Barriers: 1-2 = high (capital, regulatory), 3-4 = medium, 5 = low (technical only)
Personal Fit: 1-2 = no relevant skills, 3-4 = some skills, 5 = perfect match

rule.statement: Any score of 4 or 5 on Market Size or Competition requires a cited source URL or a "missing_data" flag. Do not accept unsupported high scores.
rule.rationale: These two dimensions are the highest fabrication-risk dimensions for LLM agents. Without a citation requirement, agents assign 4-5 to any idea they generated, producing systematically inflated totals.

rule.statement: Score ideas in batches of 10 or more. Absolute scores on single ideas are meaningless without comparison context.
rule.rationale: Isolated scores have no calibration reference. A score of 18/25 is only meaningful when compared against other candidates from the same batch.
